info.title: Sleep Scoring Web
info.description: Web API for sleep scoring and activity data analysis

get.description: Health check endpoint.
Root endpoint with API info.

Check if authentication is required.

Public endpoint - frontend uses this to determine if login is needed.

post.description:
Login with username and optional password, get session cookie.

Creates a session and sets an HTTP-only secure cookie.
Frontend should redirect here if no valid session exists.

Logout and clear session cookie.

Deletes the session from storage and clears the cookie.

schema.title: Response Session Logout Api V1 Auth Session Logout Post

get.description:
Get current session info.

Returns the username from the current session.
Requires valid session (enforced by middleware).

schema.title: Response Session Me Api V1 Auth Session Me Get

post.description:
Verify site password (stateless check).

Does not create a session - frontend should store password
and send via X-Site-Password header on subsequent requests.

Upload a CSV file for processing.

The file will be parsed, validated, and stored in the database.
Activity data will be extracted and made available for analysis.

Set replace=true to re-upload an existing file (deletes old data first).

Upload a CSV file using API key authentication.

This endpoint is designed for programmatic/pipeline uploads.
Uses X-Api-Key header for authentication instead of site password.

The file will be parsed, validated, and stored in the database.
Activity data will be extracted and made available for analysis.

get.description: List uploaded files. Users with assignments see only their files (even admins).

schema.title: Response List Files Api V1 Files Get

delete.description:
Delete all files from the database.

Optionally filter by status (e.g., 'failed' to delete only failed files).

schema.title: Response Delete All Files Api V1 Files Delete

get.description: Return current user info including admin status.

schema.title: Response Get Me Api V1 Files Auth Me Get

get.description: List all file assignments (admin only).

schema.title: Response List Assignments Api V1 Files Assignments Get

post.description: Assign files to a user (admin only). Body: {"file_ids": [...], "username": "..."}.

schema.title: Response Create Assignments Api V1 Files Assignments Post

delete.description: Remove all assignments for a user (admin only).

schema.title: Response Delete User Assignments Api V1 Files Assignments  Target Username  Delete

get.description:
Get all assignments with per-user, per-file scoring progress (admin only).

For each user, returns their assigned files with total_dates and scored_dates.
Uses the markers table (created_by) for scored dates and raw_activity_data for
total dates per file. Diary-intersected dates are used when diary exists.

schema.title: Response Get Assignment Progress Api V1 Files Assignments Progress Get

get.description: Get files with zero assignments (admin only).

schema.title: Response Get Unassigned Files Api V1 Files Assignments Unassigned Get

post.description: Delete all files whose names contain IGNORE or ISSUE.

schema.title: Response Purge Excluded Files Api V1 Files Purge Excluded Post

get.description: Get file metadata by ID.

delete.description: Delete a file and its associated data.

post.description:
Infer and persist missing File.participant_id values from filenames.

Useful for older datasets imported before participant_id inference existed.

schema.title: Response Backfill Participant Ids Api V1 Files Backfill Participant Ids Post

get.description: Get available dates for a file. If diary exists, only return study-period dates.

schema.title: Response Get File Dates Api V1 Files  File Id  Dates Get

get.description: Get dates with per-user annotation status, auto-score availability, and complexity.

schema.title: Response Get File Dates Status Api V1 Files  File Id  Dates Status Get

post.description:
Trigger batch complexity computation for all dates in a file.

Runs in background: loads activity data, runs Sadeh + Choi, loads diary,
computes complexity_pre for each date, and upserts into NightComplexity table.

schema.title: Response Compute Complexity Api V1 Files  File Id  Compute Complexity Post

get.description: Get full complexity feature breakdown for a file/date.

schema.title: Response Get Complexity Detail Api V1 Files  File Id   Analysis Date  Complexity Get

post.description:
Start a background scan of the data directory for CSV files.

Files already in the database are skipped.
Returns immediately with scan status - poll GET /scan/status for progress.

schema.title: Response Scan Data Directory Api V1 Files Scan Post

get.description:
Get the current status of the background file scan.

Poll this endpoint to track import progress.

schema.title: Response Get Scan Status Api V1 Files Scan Status Get

get.description:
Get the current status of the automatic file watcher.

The file watcher monitors the data directory for new CSV files
and automatically ingests them into the database.

schema.title: Response Get Watcher Status Api V1 Files Watcher Status Get

delete.description: Remove a single file assignment (admin only).

schema.title: Response Delete File Assignment Api V1 Files  File Id  Assignments  Target Username  Delete

get.description:
Get activity data for a specific file and date.

Returns data in columnar format for efficient transfer.
The view window starts from analysis_date at 12:00 (noon) and extends for view_hours.

Get activity data with sleep scoring algorithm results.

Returns data with:
- Sleep scoring results (1=sleep, 0=wake)
- Choi nonwear detection results (1=nonwear, 0=wear)

Available algorithms:
- sadeh_1994_actilife (default): Sadeh 1994 with ActiLife scaling
- sadeh_1994_original: Sadeh 1994 original paper version
- cole_kripke_1992_actilife: Cole-Kripke 1992 with ActiLife scaling
- cole_kripke_1992_original: Cole-Kripke 1992 original paper version

Get activity data with Sadeh algorithm results.

DEPRECATED: Use /{file_id}/{analysis_date}/score?algorithm=sadeh_1994_actilife instead.

Get all markers for a specific file and date.

Returns sleep markers, nonwear markers, and calculated metrics.
Optionally includes algorithm results for overlay display.

put.description:
Save markers for a specific file and date.

Replaces all existing markers for this file/date with the new ones.
Triggers background calculation of sleep metrics.

schema.title: Response Delete Marker Api V1 Markers  File Id   Analysis Date   Period Index  Delete

get.description:
Get sleep markers from previous and next days.

Used to show continuity of sleep across day boundaries.

post.description:
Upload nonwear sensor data CSV (study-wide).

Matches rows to activity files by participant_id column.

Expected CSV columns (case-insensitive):
- participant_id: Participant identifier (matched to activity filenames)
- date / startdate: Analysis date (YYYY-MM-DD or MM/DD/YYYY)
- start_time / nonwear_start: Nonwear start time (HH:MM)
- end_time / nonwear_end: Nonwear end time (HH:MM)

For each date+file, existing nonwear markers are replaced.
Multiple nonwear periods per date are supported (one row per period).

Upload nonwear sensor data CSV for a specific activity file (legacy).

Does not require participant_id — all rows go to the specified file.

Upload a sleep marker export CSV (desktop or web app format).

Supports both desktop export CSVs (with onset_time/offset_time) and
web export CSVs (with Onset Datetime/Offset Datetime, Study Date, etc.).

Matches rows to activity files by `filename` column, or by
`participant_id` + `timepoint` columns when no filename is present.

For each (file, date), existing sleep markers are replaced and metrics recalculated.

Expected CSV columns (comment lines starting with # are stripped):
- filename OR (numerical_participant_id + participant_timepoint)
- sleep_date / study_date / date: Analysis date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- onset_time + offset_time (HH:MM), OR onset_datetime + offset_datetime (full datetime)
- marker_type (optional): MAIN_SLEEP / NAP (default: MAIN_SLEEP)
- marker_index / period_index (optional): Period index (default: sequential)
- is_no_sleep (optional): TRUE/FALSE -- marks date as no-sleep
- needs_consensus (optional): TRUE/FALSE -- flags for consensus review
- Rows with NO_SLEEP onset/offset are treated as no-sleep dates.

get.description:
Get activity data around a marker for onset/offset tables.

Returns data points within window_minutes of the onset and offset timestamps.
Accepts optional onset_ts/offset_ts query params to use client-side timestamps
instead of requiring a saved marker in the database.

parameters.name: onset_ts

schema.description: Onset timestamp in seconds (use instead of DB lookup)
schema.title: Onset Ts

parameters.description: Onset timestamp in seconds (use instead of DB lookup)

parameters.name: offset_ts

schema.description: Offset timestamp in seconds (use instead of DB lookup)
schema.title: Offset Ts

parameters.description: Offset timestamp in seconds (use instead of DB lookup)

get.description:
Get full 48h of activity data for popout table display.

Returns all epochs from noon of analysis_date to noon of next day.
Includes algorithm results and nonwear detection.

post.description:
Start background auto-score prepopulation across complete-diary dates.

Uses complete diary rows only; incomplete diary dates are never auto-scored.

get.description: Return in-memory auto-score batch progress.

post.description:
Automatically score a date using the rule-based engine.

Returns suggestions for user to accept/reject.
Also saves results as a "auto_score" UserAnnotation for consensus comparison.

parameters.name: onset_epochs

schema.description: Min consecutive sleep epochs for onset (e.g. 3 or 5)
schema.title: Onset Epochs

parameters.description: Min consecutive sleep epochs for onset (e.g. 3 or 5)

parameters.name: offset_minutes

schema.description: Min consecutive minutes for offset (e.g. 5 or 10)
schema.title: Offset Minutes

parameters.description: Min consecutive minutes for offset (e.g. 5 or 10)

parameters.name: detection_rule

schema.description: Sleep detection rule active at time of scoring
schema.title: Detection Rule

parameters.description: Sleep detection rule active at time of scoring

get.description:
Get the auto_score user's saved annotation for this file/date.

Returns the markers in SleepPeriod format so the frontend can
accept them as the current user's own score.

schema.title: Response Get Auto Score Result Api V1 Markers  File Id   Analysis Date  Auto Score Result Get

post.description:
Automatically detect nonwear periods using diary anchors and zero-activity detection.
Returns suggestions for user to accept/reject.

parameters.name: threshold

schema.description: Max activity count to consider as zero
schema.title: Threshold

parameters.description: Max activity count to consider as zero

get.description: Return available pipeline components per role and their parameter schemas.

schema.title: Response Discover Pipeline Api V1 Markers Pipeline Discover Get

post.description: Auto-score using the configurable pipeline.

get.description:
Get list of available export columns.

Returns all available columns with their metadata, grouped by category.

post.description:
Generate CSV export for specified files.

Returns metadata about the export. Use /csv/download to get the actual file.
Download sleep marker CSV export.
Download nonwear marker CSV export (separate from sleep).

get.description:
Quick export endpoint for simple GET requests.

Uses default columns and returns CSV directly.
Get cross-file summary statistics and scoring progress.
Get all diary entries for a file, ordered by date.

schema.title: Response List Diary Entries Api V1 Diary  File Id  Get

get.description:
Get diary entry for a specific file and date.

Returns None if no diary entry exists for the given file/date.

schema.title: Response Get Diary Entry Api V1 Diary  File Id   Analysis Date  Get

put.description: Create or update a diary entry for a specific file and date.

post.description:
Upload a diary CSV file (study-wide).

Matching priority:
1) filename column (exact/stem)
2) participant_id + optional timepoint
3) unique filename token fallback

Ambiguous matches are skipped and reported (never guessed).

Upload a diary CSV for a specific activity file (legacy endpoint).

This endpoint does NOT require a participant_id column — all rows
are assigned to the specified file_id.

get.description:
Get current user's settings, merged with study-wide settings.

Study-wide settings (detection rule, night hours, filename patterns)
are shared across all users. Per-user settings (algorithm, display
preferences) are personal.

put.description:
Update current user's settings.

Creates settings record if it doesn't exist.
Only updates fields that are provided (non-None).

delete.description:
Reset user settings to defaults.

Deletes the settings record, so next GET will return defaults.

get.description: Get study-wide settings shared across all users.

put.description: Update study-wide settings. These are shared across all users.

get.description:
Get overview of all dates with 2+ user annotations.

Returns dates that need consensus review, ordered by annotation count descending.

Get all user annotations for a specific file/date.

Returns all submitted annotations plus any admin resolution.
Get vote-ready candidate marker sets with aggregate counts.

post.description:
Cast or replace vote for a consensus candidate.

One active vote per user/date. candidate_id=null clears the vote.

Admin resolves disputed annotations for a file/date.

Creates or updates the resolved annotation with the final markers.
Stores resolution separately — does NOT overwrite the main Marker table.
Main markers are preserved but the date is marked as "resolved" status
via the resolved_annotations table.

get.description: Get processing status for a file (in-memory tracker or DB fallback).

ActivityDataColumnar.title: ActivityDataColumnar
ActivityDataColumnar.description:
Columnar format for efficient JSON transfer.

This format reduces JSON overhead by using arrays instead of
repeated object keys for each data point.

SleepImportResponse.description: Response after importing sleep marker CSV (desktop or web export).

SleepMetrics.title: SleepMetrics
SleepMetrics.description:
Complete sleep quality metrics for a single sleep period.

Implements Tudor-Locke metrics algorithm as defined in the
actigraph.sleepr R package.

Reference:
    Tudor-Locke C, et al. (2014). Fully automated waist-worn accelerometer algorithm
    for detecting children's sleep-period time. Applied Physiology, Nutrition, and
    Metabolism, 39(1):53-57.

SleepPeriod.title: SleepPeriod
SleepPeriod.description:
Individual sleep period with onset/offset timestamps.

Ported from desktop's dataclasses_markers.SleepPeriod.

VoteRequest.title: VoteRequest
VoteRequest.description: Cast/replace vote for a candidate; null clears vote.
